item.title: Survived brain tumor surgery ; got broken up by an asshole bf
item.description: On December 16,2020 I had brain surgery for a tumor. Because of complications, I developed ataxia on the left side, facial paralysis, was disabled and on a wheel chair for a month, and loss of hearing on my left ear. My mental health was not good throughout this experience, and because of COVID-19, I was unable to have any visitors while I was recovering. It was very difficult and straining on me, both physically and mentally. But eventually, I was moved to rehab and was able to finally see my family and ex. Now, in the beginning, my ex was very understanding and patient with me and I was very grateful to him for it. But he wanted "sexual favors" and because how surgery left me and the trauma from it, I didn't want to. I talked with him about it and thought he understood, but I found out he was cheating on me because "he needed sex" and went looking for it elsewhere. He then broke up with me because I was "too depressing and poor" (since I lost my job) for him, so he couldn't be with me. I was very upset because he did that to me at a very difficult time, but I was able to lift myself up and move on. Now, months later, I'm stronger and better than I was before and now he's begging for me to take him back and that it was a mistake on his part. But I am truly happy. I didn't let him knock me down, I continued with rehabilitation and didn't give up no matter how hard it was, and even found someone who absolutely cares for me and treats me way better than my ex ever did. Overall, 2020/2021 was very difficult for me, but as the saying goes, "as one door closes, another opens". And now on 2022, I feel like my life is finally moving forward for the better. Thank you for listening and I hope everything goes well/gets better for you too. Good bless Tldr; struggled after brain surgery, ex cheats then dumps me, I get better, he comes begging for me back, love my life better without him.
item.dc:creator: Yanellex33

item.dc:creator: HaleyAngelOG

item.title: China is asshole
item.description: Chinese Nationalists Call for Boycott of The Matrix Resurrections After Keanu Reeves Joins Tibet Benefit Concert https://thus.org/benefitconcert/ The post Chinese Nationalists Call for Boycott of The Matrix Resurrections After Keanu Reeves Joins Tibet Benefit Concert appeared first on Consequence. Keanu Reeves was recently announced for this year’s Tibet House US benefit concert, drawing backlash from Chinese nationalists — some of whom have called for a boycott of his latest film, The Matrix Resurrections. Reeves’ support for the cultural organization, which was founded in 1987 at the request of Tibetan leader the Dalai Lama, has struck a nerve. The People’s Republic of China annexed Tibet in 1950, which ultimately led to the exile of the Dalai Lama, and the Communist Party has cracked down on the region’s people and their culture since then.
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: This is why we can't have nice things.
item.description:
Youtube is truly a wretched hive of scum an villainy. Todays example comes in the form of a "restoration" channel, that usually does bladed weaponry - knives, swords etc. Their particular claim to fame is lying about the items they restore (calling replicas "antique") and performing destructive modifications, while calling them "restorations". As expected, their unskilled, dishonest work has paid off, to the tune of 5 million subscribers. I've previously covered their "work" here: https://imgur.com/gallery/zVR4zyp
Martini variants. The martini rifle system was first adopted in 1871, went through many changes (and had many different subvariants, like carbines for artillery). I believe the rifle abused in this video was one of the carbine variants of the martini enfield, based on barrel length and barrel calibre. Image source https://www.rockislandauction.com/detail/1028/366/five-single-shot-martini-pattern-longarms
So, onto todays reckless video: What do you do if an antique martini rifle, one with shallow and minimal rust? Well, I can tell you "carefully restore it via carding and skilled gunsmithing" is apparently *not* the answer. (Rifles are outside my field, cant say if its a martini henry, martini metford, martini enfield etc, nor which revision it is)
Wood appears to be in rough shape, but by no means unrecoverable. Still has the regimental disk in the stock.
Some markings, IIRC the broad arrow is a mark of the British war office. Commonly seen on all government firearms. Again, very shallow corrosion. This is well within the realms of restoration and conservation.
Spoiler alert, things go badly from here. Oh, and that text overlay? That's from the original video. Incase anyone thought this was an innocent mistake...
Can anyone guess why the barrel might be coming off? Perhaps to convert away that shallow corrosion and check the condition of the rifling?
Ah, of course not. The only logical thing to do with an antique rifle, is turn it into a shitty replica sword. I wonder how well it will adhere to the traditional design of a "katana"?
The angle grinder, my favorite restoration tool. Definitely not destructive at all. Who cares about preserving the finite and limited number of historical artifacts out there, anyway?
Definitely not something you could have used standard bar stock or scrap steel to make, is it. Clearly you *need* to molest an antique rifle if you want some steel to make into a sword blade.
Now, call me crazy but that curvature looks rather wrong for a katana. Maybe a particularly deeply curved tachi, but even then...
And what's this? A leather covered tsuka? Where is the samegawa, the stingray skin? If you're already destroying history, I guess why not shit all over traditional materials used in katana, too.
This is a samegawa wrapped tsuka. This is how it should look. Image from https://www.cottontailcustoms.com/tsukamaki/ - which contains a very detailed guide on how to do this whole process correctly. Which they obviously did not bother to google.
I guess alternating ito wrap is too much to expect from a content farm. Why bother doing things correctly, it's clear your viewers don't care about history, accuracy or authenticity.
Alternating ito. How it should be done. Again, from https://www.cottontailcustoms.com/tsukamaki/
And how it looks done right: tight, even diamonds, alternating directions between each rung of the wrapping, nice nodules of stingray skin underneath. Image source is still, surprise surprise, https://www.cottontailcustoms.com/tsukamaki/
To save us all the pain, I have skipped over most of the forging of this "Katana" And what's this? a shamshir blade mounted on a loose approximation of a katana hilt? Not even a hamon? A martini rifle died *for this?* Disgraceful.
item.dc:creator: IPostSwords

item.title: *Cue X-Files Theme*
item.description:
Stole these from Buzzfeed...(who stole them from Reddit)
I've also had that happen a couple of times. It's always very brief, and random - seeing a specific scene for a split second, that I had dreamed recently and somehow remembered. (I usually forget most of my dreams). Weirds me out.
item.dc:creator: ButtersTheCat

item.title: Sometimes you just have to be "that annoying guy" to get what you need!
item.description: Told my doctor for nearly 1 year my back was killing Me! I couldn't tie my shoes, go to the toilet normal. One day i just started yelling like a mad man at him. "I need a scanning of my back! I can't live like this no more! So he gave me the "go"... What do you know i have a disc herniation.. never have a i seen a man so little.. 1 year if him telling me " it's a muscle, a nerve. You slept wrong... (Sorry for the long text.. i just want to let you know.. if you know somethings wrong.. you know your body best) Jesus! most Viral! thank you very much! and thank you for sending your storys about back/spine pain. its a huuuge problem for so many of us. Lots of love. (send dogs.. i do love dogs)
item.dc:creator: flamingbackpain

item.title: Weird Wednesday Video Dump: Now with 38% more animals! (Sound on)
item.description:
Welcome to a late night Weird Wednesday Dump. Or maybe it's more Wacky than weird. Maybe we'll never truly know.
Wi-Fi on, it's a biggin'
Something something Tony Danza
Life be this way these days
You have now reached the part of this post featuring squirrels.
The squirrel section is now concluded
ANGERY
Quick, to the basement of the Alamo!
What a little helper
"Ah fwick!" -baby, probably
Jesus!
Love this movie. And Roger Miller
Me in quarantine
Be careful he bite
Run cat!! Oh.
From the old MadTV talent show sketch
He ded
True. I hate my wife's wakeup ringtone
That boy ain't right
You are all weirdos...
...but you are my kind of weirdos. That makes us friends, right?
Remember, always pullin for ya

item.dc:creator: SisterMidnight

item.title: I make historical clothing by hand
item.description:
This is the first dress I sewed entirely by hand. It is based on dresses from the 1360s in Northern Europe. It is made from wool and, if I may say so myself, fits like a glove.
This is a high status viking age dress that I finished this summer. Again, everything is hand sewn and based on archeological finds from the timeperiod. The fabrics were plant dyed by a friend of mine. I'm currently waiting for a pair of custom made oval broaches that will be attatched to the straps of the blue dress.
I make socks and mittens to my historical garments using an old technique called needle binding. The yarn was hand spun by another friend.
This is my viking age pesant dress. I made it from a thicker wool, naturally brown. I wear it often when I'm doing viking camping. It is warm and durable.
I tried blacksmithing for the first time this summer. Made an S-hook for my cooking pot. If you like reenactment and/or viking age stuff I have a YouTube channel called VikingVentures. Here is a link to one of my videos. https://youtu.be/cdLfPP4tPK0 Stay weird and wonderful Imgur!
item.dc:creator: SwedishPunkAndBeer
